content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Gilles Lambert on UnsplashOver the course of this series, we’ve explored nearly every major aspect of mobile application security in Flutter.
We learned how to:

• Authenticate users securely

• Store sensitive data safely

• Protect APIs with JWTs

• Refresh expired tokens

• Encrypt local databases

• Secure network communication with HTTPS and TLS

• Strengthen connections with certificate pinning

• Understand Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks

• Protect against reverse engineering

• Detect rooted and jailbroken devices

• Detect emulators and runtime tampering

• Reduce the impact of APK repackaging

• Design secure logging strategies
Each topic solved a specific security problem.
But here’s the important question:
How do all these pieces work together in a real application?
Security isn’t about adding one feature.
It’s about combining multiple layers so that if one control fails, others continue…
